lesson: Aquarium debug UI uses a code-first Direct2D/DirectWrite overlay with explicit bound controls. Rect math must distinguish edges from width/height, controls need base/hover/active feedback, tooltips live in declarations, and long mode selectors should be named option rows instead of numeric sliders.
Renderer presentation knobs belong in a typed CultCache graphics settings document, not process folklore. Current durable knobs are render debug mode, exposure, bloom intensity, and bloom veil.
Use the MathWorld teardrop curve for the cursor profile with m=6, revolved into an SDF locator. Keep ripples as geometry, material as brass GGX metal, and avoid ambient fill.

Runtime volumetrics, cloud/fog research, and the D3D11 scene renderer are shelved because they distract from Aquarium's Epiphany-client mission. Git history holds the experiment; live repo state should not preserve compatibility husks or dead guidance for removed medium settings.

Medium/froxel render targets, structured buffers, shader bindings, scene outputs, post history lanes, and medium debug paths were removed from the live D3D12 renderer. Future atmospheric or field transport work needs a new explicit pass contract rather than reviving shelved scaffolding.
Blue-noise assets are retained for future dithering. Self and future other Selves emit through an explicit body light buffer; generic field instances are not the lighting authority.

Studio HDR reflection lighting is baked as a PMREM DDS cubemap: roughness selects higher mips, and the bake samples the source latlong by output direction so cubemap faces are generated from one continuous domain instead of filtered as isolated square images.

The Studio3 PMREM asset is live in the scene as a D3D12 TextureCube bound at t22. The renderer owns a narrow RGBA16F DX10 DDS cubemap loader, keeps upload resources alive until the copy fence completes, and the scene shader uses roughness-scaled SampleLevel for specular material reflections only.

Grid linework was replaced with a terminating perfect mirror surface. Grid hits now write the primary scene packet with FIELD_ID_GRID, terrain normal, full coverage, and Studio3 PMREM mip 0 reflection instead of using the transparent event lane.

Aquarium world space is Z-up while the baked Studio3 PMREM cubemap is sampled as Y-up. Every PMREM lookup must convert world directions with (x, z, y) before TextureCube sampling, or the HDRI appears sideways.

Active reflection probe switched to Studio2 from E:\Projects\Aetheria-Economy\Assets\Textures\studio2.hdr. The renderer loads Assets/Textures/studio2_pmrem.dds through the existing D3D12 TextureCube PMREM path, and the unused Studio3 DDS was cut from live assets.

Self's Grid gravity bowl now uses a 17-world-unit radius and an Aetheria-style radial cosine wave. The brush equation is mirrored across D3D12Grid, CPU body-light placement, D3D12Scene body centers, and D3D12Post temporal reprojection; body centers sit at Grid height plus 2x radius.
Grid reflection roughness is now slope-driven: flat regions sample higher Studio2 PMREM mips to avoid an over-literal HDRI mirror, while sloped or waved regions sharpen toward mip 0.

Flat Grid reflection now tops out at Studio2 PMREM mip 3, and ray misses use Studio2 PMREM mip 3 as the soft background so the reflective Grid is not contrasted against black void. Low-resolution mip texels are hidden with a small multi-direction cone average rather than one magnified TextureCube sample.
Studio2 PMREM was rebaked at 512px per cubemap face with 10 mips, so mip 3 is 64px per face instead of 32px. Shader roughness LOD max moved to 9 to match the chain.

Diffuse IBL is not GGX PMREM with a softer mip. Studio2 diffuse lighting now uses a separate irradiance cubemap baked by cosine-weighted hemisphere convolution, stores the Li*cos(theta) irradiance integral, and shades non-metal bodies with Fresnel-gated albedo/PI diffuse response.

Rider may open Aquarium.Engine.slnx with empty project trees even though dotnet can build it. Keep Aquarium.Engine.sln as the Rider-facing classic solution while Aquarium.Engine.slnx remains the compact CLI solution.

Epiphany agent bodies should be role-specific SDF organs driven by published CultNet surfaces: identity from epiphany.agent_memory, motion from epiphany.agent_heartbeat, posture from surface roles/coordinator/role_result, Face output from surface.face, and continuity pressure from pressure/reorient surfaces. Body and Imagination are the first implementation pair because they exercise grounded graph mass and generative harmonic bloom against the same AgentVisualState contract.

Agent orbit lanes are home anchors, not cages. Role bodies may wander a bounded distance from their designated orbit slots for expression, and vertical animation should come from CPU-visible gravity-well pulses so Grid height, body centers, shadows, and temporal reprojection share the same placement truth.
